name: opentldr

metadata.name: opentldr
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: An open framework for creation of a Tailored Daily Report.

documentation:
# OpenTLDR-Core An Open Framework for Generating an Tailored Daily Report This repository contains the OpenTLDR package. You can 'pip install opentldr' to get the contents of this repository as a python package in your virtaul env. ![overview image](https://opentldr.org/images/opentldr.png) The latest OpenTLDR-Core library is availible as a Pip Package: 
python3 -m pip install opentldr
## Introduction to OpenTLDR OpenTLDR is an open source framework written in Python to implement the process of creating a Tailored Daily Report from a series of news-like articles. This repository contains the Core classes used as a library by OpenTLDR implementations, like the example notebooks you can find in the OpenTLDR-Example repo on GitHub. OpenTLDR is composed of Python modules: - A **KnowledgeGraph** module that has been implemented as a layer on top of the Neo4j Graph Database - A module with a set of **Domain** classes that implement the nodes and edges in a TLDR-focused knowledge graph - A **Workflow** module and tool that wraps the execution of a series of Python Notebooks to make them behave like components - A **DataRepo** module that abstracts the data ingest from Python Notebooks using a config parameter for either S3 or Files This GitHub repo handles the back-end code that is only required if you wish to modify this functionality directly. Otherwise, you should use the OpenTDLR-Example repository, which implements analytic pieces of the process in a series of Jupyter Notebooks. All of the code in this repo is available to Python code as a pip library using a command like `python3 -m pip install opentldr` please make sure that you need to modify this library directly before using this codebase. ## KnowledgeGraph OpenTLDR uses a Neo4j graph database as the storage layer of its KnowledgeGraph. The default (and easiest) way to do this is to run the Community Edition neo4j Server in a Docker container on the local machine that you execute OpenTLDR workflows. A linux shell script (start_neo4j.sh) and docker-compose configuraiton file (scripts/Neo4j/docker-compose) automate this process. The default container does not require authentication but only connects to localhost loopback interface. You can change all of this and use .env or system environment variables to direct the OpenTLDR library to use the desired neo4j server and user credientials. OpenTLDR uses the KnowledgeGraph's API to build up a series of objects and relationships (see the Domain module) over a series of steps in the automated workflow (see the Workflow module). This includes loading content (e.g., news articles), which means that the graph database can become large. The KnowledgeGraph API is designed to be verb-oriented and functional, with the Domain classes being the nouns and providing useful data objects. The primary reason for this API design was to keep the Notebooks simple, clean, and transactional to the KnowledgeGraph. The default query methods return either a single or list of objects defined by a Domain class. ## Domain The OpenTLDR library includes class definitions for all of the objects represented in the KnowledgeGraph. This standardizes the graph, so that multiple analytic workflows can easily achieve compatability. Each Domain class includes the following properties: - uid - a text string that acts as a unique identifier that is created the first time the data object is saved and can be queried. - meta - a json object that can be used to decorate any node or edge with additional properties that your workflow might use (but that are not expected to be implemented by others) The schema of the KnowledgeGraph is represented graphically to indicate how the different node and edge types interconnect. As long as the Domain objects and KnowledgeGraph API are used, it should be difficult to end up with incorrect connections. Every edge type is also a uniquely named Domain object with a uid and meta properties. ![schema image](https://opentldr.org/images/schema.png) The colors in this image can be replicated in the neo4j interface using the `scripts/neo4j_styles.grass` file, which can be drag and dropped on the main neo4j webpage. Note that the naming convention for neo4j suggests that nodes be labeled with upper camel case capitalization without spaces (which is the same as the naming of the python Domain classes) and edges are all caps with underlines as spaces (which can sometimes be confusing when switching between the python Domain class names and the labels in the graph and cypher code). ## Workflow The OpenTLDR Workflow uses PaperMill to execute the sequences of analytic notebooks and parameterize them. This should not impede the notebooks from being run manually when desired, but it does enforce the expected workflow order of execution that should be used when running evaluation scoring for an end-to-end analytic workflow. ### The Default Workflow Parameters There are a few paramters that are used throughout the standard workflow. #### Output Folder This indicates where the automated workflow should place the outputed Notebooks. Note that these are copies of the original notebooks that show the execution results. They copies are overwritten each time the workflow is executed. The original notebooks should always be treated as read-only copies. 
output_folder = "./READ_ONLY_OUTPUT"
#### Notebook Order This parameter is a list of strings, where each string is the relative path of a Notebook file to run. We tend to name these with the order that they are executed manually, but the `Workflow` class always executes in the order they appear in this list. 
notebook_order = [
    "Step_0_Initialize.ipynb",
  ...
    "Step_6_Evaluate.ipynb"
    ]
If you wish to change the running order or add/remove steps, you can do so by editing this list. #### Variables to Set This parameter is of type dict that is converted into additional parameters that are injected into the notebooks at run-time. The key value will become the variable name. 
variables_to_set={
    "message": "Successfully passed in parameters from Workflow.ipynb!",
   ...
    "repo_config": {'repo_type': 'files', 'path': './sample_data'}
    }
Note that this format is likely to change to better support passing different parameters into individual steps. ## DataRepo The DataRepo class provides an abstraction for locating data to use within the steps of the workflow. For example, in the above `variables_to_set` specification, there is a config json string `repo_config` that indicates to pull data from a directory of files located at the given path. At this time, only files and S3 buckets are implemented: Config for using files, in this case from the local sample_data folder: 
repo_config = {
        'repo_type': 'files',
        'path': './sample_data'
        }
Config for using S3 Bucket where the bucket name, access key id, and s3 secret are pulled from environment variables: 
repo_config = {
        'repo_type': 's3',
        'bucket': os.getenv("S3_BUCKET"),
        'aws_access_key_id': os.getenv("S3_ACCESS_KEY_ID"),
        'aws_secret_access_key': os.getenv("S3_SECRET_KEY")
        }
Below is an example of using the DataRepo to load news content from either of the above: 
from opentldr import KnowledgeGraph, DataRepo

kg = KnowledgeGraph()
cr = DataRepo(kg, repo_config)

for uid in cr.importContentData():
    print ("Loaded Content: {uid}".format(uid=uid))
This will create a DataRepo `cr` that writes to the KnowledgeGraph `kg` from the parsed Content configured above. The return value is a list of the uids of Content nodes created (note Source nodes would be created if needed but not returned here).

classes.name: opentldr.ContentEnrichment.OrderedEdges
documentation: Document Content

classes.name: opentldr.ContentEnrichment.Image
documentation: Image Content

classes.name: opentldr.ContentEnrichment.AudioChunk
documentation: Audio Content

classes.name: opentldr.ContentEnrichment.Scene
documentation: Video Content

classes.name: opentldr.ContentEnrichment.SimilarTo

classes.name: opentldr.DataRepo.DataRepo
documentation: Factory class to setup any type of DataRepo from the configuration inputs.

classes.name: opentldr.DataRepoJson.DataRepoJsonFiles
documentation:
Reads a single .json text file from the path and populates the KG using it.
This is a great way to export checkpoints for a KG and re-import them later.

classes.name: opentldr.AbstractDataRepo.AbstractDataRepo

methods.name: opentldr.AbstractDataRepo.AbstractDataRepo.parseAnnotatedText
documentation:
Parses a string from an annotated text file, extracts the annotations, and
        returns a tuple (dict of metadata , string of non annotation text block).

        Annotations are commented property key : value pairs, such as:
            # uid = 1234

            This is a text block.

            # name = Chris

            Another line.

            And one at the end.


        Will return ({'uid': '1234', 'name':'Chris}, 'This is a text block.
Another line.
And one at the end')
baseclass for all Data Repositories, includes shared methods and ingest ordering.

classes.name: opentldr.FileSystemDataRepo.FileSystemDataRepo

methods.name: opentldr.FileSystemDataRepo.FileSystemDataRepo._importByClass
documentation: TODO: Move repeated method to superclass

Reads all the text files in the provided directory path. Creates a Source node for the directory and parses the text files.
This extracts lines with property definitions (e.g., "date: 05/14/1960 08:00PM") and puts the rest into the text body.
Creates a Content node, for each file and adds it to the knowledge graph.

methods.name: opentldr.Workflow.Workflow.verify
documentation: Walk thru the structure of the workflow and ensure it is setup correctly.

methods.name: opentldr.Workflow.Workflow.ensure_path
documentation: Ensure_path(path) simply creates a directory if it is not there already.

methods.name: opentldr.Workflow.Workflow.execute_notebook
documentation: Execute_notebook runs a single notebook file in PaperMill returns the run time in seconds

methods.name: opentldr.Workflow.Workflow.run
documentation: Run the defined workflow files in order in PaperMill

functions.name: opentldr.Domain.jsonToKg
documentation:
Hydrate a json string to a Domain object. This requires the "class" attribute to
determine the correct class type, so it only works on these classes.

classes.name: opentldr.Domain.OpenTldrMeta

methods.name: opentldr.Domain.OpenTldrMeta.to_text
documentation: to_text() returns a unique, user readable string representing the object.

OpenTldrMeta is a mixin class that adds a unique id (uid) and JSON property (metadata)
to a Node or Edge. This is used in every Edge and Node in OpenTLDR and provides an
interface for PyDantic for data manipulation and JSON.
UTILITY MIX-IN CLASSES

classes.name: opentldr.Domain.OpenTldrNode

methods.name: opentldr.Domain.OpenTldrNode.to_json_connect
documentation: Override to_json_connect to insert edge data into the node.

methods.name: opentldr.Domain.OpenTldrNode.from_json_connect
documentation: Override from_json_connect to rebuild edge data from serialized node.
OpenTldrNode is mixed in to each node class to support json import/export.

classes.name: opentldr.Domain.OpenTldrEdge

methods.name: opentldr.Domain.OpenTldrEdge.to_json_connect
documentation: Override to_json_connect to insert edge data into the node.

methods.name: opentldr.Domain.OpenTldrEdge.from_json_connect
documentation: Override from_json_connect to rebuild edge data from serialized node.
OpenTldrEdge is mixed in to each edge class to support json import/export

classes.name: opentldr.Domain.OpenTldrText
documentation:
OpenTldrText is a mixin class that adds the properties of text and type (both are Strings)
to any Node or Edge. This is used whenever the client stores text in the knowledge graph.

classes.name: opentldr.Domain.CitableNode
documentation:
CitableNode is a baseclass for any Node from which we extract entities (e.g., NER)
and we need to refer back to the Node from where they were detected.

classes.name: opentldr.Domain.Uncertain
documentation:
Uncertain is a mixin class that adds a confidence value (0.0-1.0) to a Node or Edge.
This is implemented to store the float value and generate description qualitative
text clauses for the ranges of this confidence value.

classes.name: opentldr.Domain.Scored
documentation:
Scored is a mixin class that adds a score value (0.0-1.0) to a Node or Edge.
This is implemented to store the float value and generate descriptive qualitative
text clauses for the ranges of this score value.

classes.name: opentldr.Domain.ReferenceEdge
documentation:
ReferenceEdge allows for general reference data relationships to be introduced into the 
knowledge graph and used abstractly (e.g., ontological distance using path finding).
This uses the OpenTldrText mixin to implement text and type properties.
REFERENCE KNOWLEDGE

classes.name: opentldr.Domain.ReferenceNode
documentation:
ReferenceNode allows for general reference data nodes to be introduced into the
knowledge graph and used abstractly (e.g., to relate extracted entities to known things).
This uses the OpenTldrText mixin to implement text and type properties.
The hypothesized property is a boolean that indicates if this was inferred (e.g., discovered)
or provided as fact via external reference data (e.g., imported).

classes.name: opentldr.Domain.Source
documentation:
Source nodes indicate the generator of various content. This may be an author or distributor
of information. The name property is used to uniquely identify the Source.
Active Data

classes.name: opentldr.Domain.IsFrom
documentation: IsFrom edges connect Content nodes to the Source nodes in the knowledge graph.

classes.name: opentldr.Domain.Content
documentation:
Content nodes represent text-based information, such as a news article or message.
They include properties for title (String), date (datetime.date), and url (String).
In OpenTldr Content node urls are used for hyperlinks back to the original material.

classes.name: opentldr.Domain.MentionedIn
documentation:
MentionedIn edges connects an extracted Entity node to the CitableNode (e.g., Content 
or Request) that included the reference.

classes.name: opentldr.Domain.RefersTo
documentation:
RefersTo edges connects an extracted Entity node to the ReferenceNode that it is believed
to represent with the uncertainity value provided.

classes.name: opentldr.Domain.Entity
documentation:
Entity nodes represent specific things that are "mentioned_in" in Citable nodes 
(e.g., Content or Requests) that "refers_to" some ReferenceNode information. The text property
contains the text for the entity identified and the type property expresses what it is.
The "refers_to" edge can be uncertain (e.g., sematic similarity of text and type consistency).

classes.name: opentldr.Domain.User
documentation:
User node represents a client of the OpenTLDR system and has name and email properties (Stings).
Similar to a Content connecting to a Source, Requests connect to a User (see RequestedBy edge).
Information Request

classes.name: opentldr.Domain.RequestedBy
documentation: RequestedBy edges connect Request nodes to the User node to reflect who made the request.

classes.name: opentldr.Domain.Request
documentation:
Request nodes information requests that are made by users to indicate what information they
are interested in and how to tailor that information for them. A "text" property (String) 
holds the text of the information request and the "title" property (String) provides a 
short labels for the request to avoid repeating long request text. The "requested_by" edge
links each request to one User node.

classes.name: opentldr.Domain.Recommends
documentation: Recommends edge connects a Recommendation node to the Content node that is being recommended.
Workflow Products | Recommendations and Summaries

classes.name: opentldr.Domain.RelatesTo
documentation: RelatesTo edge connects a Recommendation node to the Request on which it is based.

classes.name: opentldr.Domain.Recommendation
documentation:
Recommendation nodes represent the assertion that a Content node (pointed to by Recommends edge)
is believed to be relevant to a Request node (pointed to by the RelatesTo edge). The score
property (float 0.0 - 1.0) indicates how relevant and thus how strong the recommendation.

classes.name: opentldr.Domain.Summarizes
documentation: Summerizes edges connect a Summary node to the Content node that they summarize.

classes.name: opentldr.Domain.FocusOn
documentation:
FocusOn edges connect a Summary node to the Recommendation node that may help inform what
the most relevant parts of the Summary are with respect to that Recommendation / Request.

classes.name: opentldr.Domain.Summary
documentation:
Summary node contains text (String property) that is a shortened (ideally tailored) version
of the Content node connect with the "summaries" edge. The "focus_on" edge connects to the
Recommendation that may inform how this summary is tailored.

classes.name: opentldr.Domain.Includes
documentation:
Includes edges connect a TldrEntry node to a Summary node to indicate that the Summary is
to be used in that part of the TLDR.
Workflow Products | TLDR

classes.name: opentldr.Domain.BasedOn
documentation:
BasedOn edges connect a TldrEntry node to a Recommendation node to indicate why that
entry is being included in the TLDR and may be used for things like ordering the entries
to show the most recommended first.

classes.name: opentldr.Domain.TldrEntry
documentation:
TldrEntry is one record within a TLDR. In general, there is a one-to-one relationship
between each TldrEntry, Recommendation, and Summary such that each record within a TLDR
has a score for the Recommendation, a text blurb from the Summary, and a connection back
to the original Content (from both the Recommendation and Summary).

classes.name: opentldr.Domain.Contains
documentation:
Connects a Tldr to each of the TldrEntry objects that makes it up. The assumption
here is that the score property (float 0.0 - 1.0) on the edge provides a descending ordering for
the entries connected by these links.

classes.name: opentldr.Domain.ResponseTo
documentation: ResponseTo edges connect a Tldr to the Request for which it was generated.

classes.name: opentldr.Domain.Tldr
documentation:
Tldr nodes represent an instance of the TLDR report for the date (in 'date' property of type
datetime.date). It is connected to the Request by the ResponseTo edge and a set of TldrEntries
by "Entries" edges.

classes.name: opentldr.Domain.FeedbackForEntry
documentation: Edges connect a Feedback to the TLDR Entry for which it was generated.
FEEDBACK

classes.name: opentldr.Domain.FeedbackForRequest
documentation: Edges connect a Feedback to the Request for which it was generated.

classes.name: opentldr.Domain.Feedback
documentation:
Feedback relates events back to a Request node to TldrEntry node to provide a method for
storing events (for example giving it stars or logging accessing the source article)

Feedback score ("score" property is float with expected range of 0.0 - 1.0) indicating
how relevant that entry is with respect to the request. These edges are generally created
by the UI when the user rates an entry (e.g., clicking on a star rating).

Feedback clicked ("clicked" property is a date) indicates when the last time the source
link for the TLDR entry was clicked by the user.

classes.name: opentldr.Domain.KeyForContent
documentation: Edges connect a EvaluationKey to a Content node.
EVALUATION

classes.name: opentldr.Domain.KeyForRequest
documentation: Edges connect a EvaluationKey to a Request.

classes.name: opentldr.Domain.EvalKey
documentation:
EvalKey is an indication of the relationship between a Content (e.g., a news article) and a
Request (i.e., what the user is interested in). This represents a "correct" tailoring pair.
This also includes a score and text to help identify what was most relevant in the pair.

functions.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph._getenv
documentation:
_getenv(variable, default) attempts to resolve the value of the environment variable specified.
If it cannot find the variable in the OS or .env (from dotenv package) it will fail over to the
provided default value while giving a warning to the logging system.

classes.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.neo4j_query
documentation:
Performs a cypher call to the knowledge base and returns results in the standard neo4j driver format.
Use this if you are following neo4j documentation, otherwise use cypher_query().
General Utility Methods

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.neomodel_query
documentation:
Performs a cypher query in the standard neomodel method, which will return a list of lists of neomodel.
Use this if you are following Neomodel documentation, otherwise use cypher_query().

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.connect
documentation: Ensures that a connection to Neo4j has been established and is functioning.

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.getNeo4jDriver
documentation:
Returns the Neo4j driver class being used (usually neo4j.GraphDatabase.driver) so that direct neo4j calls can be
made or it can be passed into other libraries.

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.close
documentation:
Shuts down the current connection to neo4j. This should be done at the end of each program / notebook.
If you use the "with statement" context control, this will be called automatically when the code block ends.

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph._ensure_saved
documentation:
OpenTLDR objects all inherit from the OpenTldrMeta mixin class. They will be assigned a "uid" when they are saved.
So, we can use this to determine if an object has been saved or not and respond appropriately.

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.get_all_node_uids_by_tag
documentation: Returns the list of uids for the tag specified as a string.

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.get_all_nodes_by_tag
documentation: Returns the list of uids for the tag specified as a string.

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.cypher_query
documentation:
Performs a cypher query and returns a single simple list of Neomodel objects based on the variable indicated (or first).
Use this for most OpenTLDR queries because it is much simpler to process for iterating on the results.
For example: cypher_query("MATCH (a)-[b]->(c) RETURN a,b,c","a") only returns a list of only the "a" results.
So, cypher_query("MATCH (a)-[b]->(c) RETURN a","a") would give the same result list by dropping the "b" and "c" results.
Reduce the risk of Cypher injection attacks by passing Cypher parameters in params.
KnowedgeGraph API

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.cypher_query_one
documentation:
Performs a query that is intended to LIMIT the return values to one single Neomodel object. This simplifies the processing
of the query results to consistently only get a single object for the extract_variable, it is undefined which unless you sort them.
For example: cypher_query_one("MATCH (a)-[b]->(c) RETURN a,b,c","a") returns the FIRST object of result "a".
So, cypher_query_one("MATCH (a)-[b]->(c) RETURN a LIMIT 1","a") gives the same result (faster).
Reduce the risk of Cypher injection attacks by passing Cypher parameters in params.

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.cypher_import
documentation: Processes a text file containing cypher commands and executes them on the KnowledgeGraph.

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.delete_all
documentation: Clears the neo4j graph database.

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.shortest_path
documentation:
This is a special cypher command to run neo4j's shortest path algorithm and return the path (list of edges) found.
We remove EvalKey, Recommendation, Tldr, Source, and User nodes to avoid irrelevant paths that circumvent Reference Data.

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.add_reference_node
documentation: Creates a new ReferenceNode object in KG.
Reference Data API
# Reference Nodes in KG are labeled as ReferenceNode

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.get_reference_node_by_uid
documentation: Returns a single ReferenceNode as indicated by the uid or None if it does not exist in KG.

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.get_all_reference_nodes
documentation: Returns a list of all the ReferenceNodes in the KG.

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.update_reference_node
documentation: Updates the KG with changes to a ReferenceNode properties

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.delete_reference_node
documentation: Removes the passed ReferenceNode and the edges connected to them.

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.delete_all_reference_nodes
documentation: Removes all of the ReferenceNode and the edges connected to them.

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.get_reference_node_by_uid
documentation: Returns a single ReferenceNode as indicated by the uid or None if it does not exist in KG.

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.get_all_reference_nodes
documentation: Returns a list of all the ReferenceNodes in the KG.

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.add_reference_edge
documentation: Adds a ReferenceEdge between two ReferenceNodes. Note these edges are only between ReferenceNodes.
# Reference Edges - in KG these are labeled as REFERENCE_EDGE but Neomodel classes are ReferenceEdge

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.get_reference_edge_by_uid
documentation: Returns a single ReferenceEdge as indicated by the uid or None if it does not exist in KG.

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.get_all_reference_edges
documentation: Returns a list of all of the ReferenceEdges in the KG.

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.update_reference_edge
documentation: Updates the KG with changes to a ReferenceEdge's properties

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.delete_reference_edge
documentation: Removes the ReferenceEdge.

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.delete_all_reference_edges
documentation: Removes all of the ReferenceEdges from KG.

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.add_source
documentation: Creates new Source node, or if one by the same name already exists it reuses it.
Active Data
Source Nodes - refer to the origin of content

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.get_source_by_uid
documentation: Gets an existing Source node by uid or None if it does not exist

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.get_source_by_name
documentation: Gets an existing Source node by name or None if it does not exist

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.update_source
documentation: Updates the KG with changes to a Source's properties.

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.delete_source
documentation: Removes the passed Source node, its content nodes, and the edges connected to it.

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.delete_all_sources
documentation: Removes all of the Sources, their content, and the edges connected to them.

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.add_content
documentation: Content Nodes - contain article content and link to their Source

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.add_entity
documentation: Active Data / Entity

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.add_user
documentation: Requests API
Users

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.add_request
documentation: Requests

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.add_recommendation
documentation: Workflow Products API
Recommendations

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.add_summary
documentation: Summaries

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.add_tldr
documentation: Tldr

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.add_entry_to_tldr
documentation: Tldr Entries

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.add_feedback_click
documentation: Feedback

methods.name: opentldr.KnowledgeGraph.KnowledgeGraph.add_evalkey
documentation: EvalKey Nodes - rubric for evaluation metrics

KnowledgeGraph is the API for OpenTLDR's interface to Neo4j graph database. It attempts to make the
driver connection using OS, .env, and then default values for connections. This API maps neo4j
nodes/edges to types specified in the Domain module (using Neomodel OGM and some custom cypher).

In order to be successful, you will need to have a neo4j server running, reachable on the network,
and with environment variables (either in OS or .env files) similar to these:

    NEO4J_CONNECTION='neo4j://localhost:7687'
    NEO4J_USERNAME=neo4jUser
    NEO4J_PASSWORD=neo4jPassword
    NEO4J_DATABASE=neo4j

This class can be used in several different ways, including:
    (1) import opentldr.KnowledgeGraph ... kg=KnowledgeGraph() ... (use kg here) ... kg.close() 
    (2) with opentldr.KnowledgeGraph as kg: ... (use kg here, and then it will autoclose)
